parts.text: Before you sleep tonight, ask yourself gently—what did I justify today that tomorrow I might regret? Not to punish yourself, just to notice. Maren never became rich and never became a thief, she became something rarer: awake. So tell me truly in the comments—what is the one thing you are still carrying that you swore was only “this once”? This story ends here, but the question does not. If this made you pause — even for a moment — you belong here. Subscribe to The Mind of Buddha.
